title: Java Cheatsheet - Hinglish
description: Java ko samjho bilkul dost ki tarah — simple aur yaadgaar examples ke saath

concepts.name: Pehla Java Program
concepts.explanation: Socho ye Java ki 'welcome ceremony' hai. Har program mein ek 'class' chahiye (ye tumhare code ka ghar hai) aur ek 'main method' (ye darwaza hai jahan se Java andar aata hai). Class ek building hai, main() uska main gate hai.
keyPoints: public = koi bhi access kar sakta hai (jaise public park)
static = ye class ka hai, kisi specific object ka nahi
void = ye method kuch return nahi karta
main = Java isi naam ko dhundta hai program start karne ke liye
String[] args = optional inputs jo program run karte waqt de sakte ho

concepts.name: Primitive Data Types
concepts.explanation: Ye Java ke building blocks hain — simple values jo directly memory mein store hoti hain. Socho jaise alag-alag size ke dabbon ki tarah: byte sabse chhota dabba hai, int medium, long sabse bada. Memory bachane ke liye sabse chhota dabba use karo jo kaam kar jaye.
concepts.code:
// Whole numbers (size ke hisaab se choose karo)
byte chhota = 127;          // -128 se 127 (1 byte) - jaise match ki dibbi
short medium = 32000;       // -32,768 se 32,767 (2 bytes)
int aam = 2_000_000;        // Sabse common choice (4 bytes) - daily use
long bahut_bada = 9_000_000_000L; // 'L' lagana mat bhoolna! (8 bytes)

// Decimal numbers
float price = 19.99f;       // 'f' lagao! (4 bytes) - approx values
double sahi = 3.14159;      // Default decimal ke liye (8 bytes) - precise

// Others
boolean ready = true;       // Sirf true ya false - haan ya na
char letter = 'A';          // Single character single quotes mein
keyPoints: int tumhara go-to hai whole numbers ke liye — 99% time kaafi hai
double default hai decimals ke liye — float tab hi use karo jab memory tight ho
L lagana mat bhoolna long ke liye aur f float ke liye — compiler ko hint chahiye!
Badi numbers mein underscore use karo readability ke liye: 1_000_000 = 1000000

concepts.name: User Input Lena
concepts.explanation: Scanner tumhare program ka mic hai — ye sunta hai jo user type kare. Import karo, System.in se connect karo (keyboard), aur alag methods use karo based on kya expect kar rahe ho. Kaam hone ke baad band karna mat bhoolna!
concepts.code:
import java.util.Scanner;  // Step 1: Import karo

Scanner scanner = new Scanner(System.in);  // Step 2: Banao

System.out.print("Tumhara naam? ");  // print (same line pe)
String naam = scanner.nextLine();  // Puri line padho

System.out.print("Tumhari umar? ");
int umar = scanner.nextInt();       // Sirf number padho

scanner.close();  // Step 3: Band karo jab ho jaye!
keyPoints: nextLine() = Enter tak sab kuch padho (naam, sentence ke liye best)
nextInt()/nextDouble() = sirf number padho, Enter chhod deta hai
Common bug fix: nextInt() ke baad nextLine() lagao leftover Enter ke liye
print() same line pe rehta hai, println() next line pe jaata hai

concepts.name: Arithmetic Operators
concepts.explanation: Ye basic math operations hain — jaise calculator mein hote hain. Plus, minus, multiply, divide, aur modulo (remainder).
concepts.code:
int a = 10, b = 3;
int jod = a + b;        // 13 (addition)
int ghata = a - b;      // 7 (subtraction)
int guna = a * b;       // 30 (multiplication)
int bhaag = a / b;      // 3 (integer division - decimal kata jaata hai)
int bacha = a % b;      // 1 (modulo - remainder jo bache)

a++; // Increment: a = 11 (1 badh gaya)
b--; // Decrement: b = 2 (1 kam ho gaya)
keyPoints: / se integer division hota hai agar dono numbers integer hon — decimal kat jaata hai
% modulo deta hai (jo bache wo) — even/odd check karne mein helpful
++ se 1 badhta hai, -- se 1 kam hota hai

concepts.name: Comparison Operators
concepts.explanation: Do values ko compare karo aur true/false lo. Jaise exam mein marks compare karte hain.
concepts.code:
int x = 5, y = 10;
boolean barabar = (x == y);      // false (kya dono equal hain?)
boolean alag = (x != y);         // true (kya dono different hain?)
boolean bada = (x > y);          // false (kya x bada hai?)
boolean chhota = (x < y);        // true (kya x chhota hai?)
boolean badaYaBabar = (x >= 5);  // true (bada ya equal)
boolean chhotaYaBabar = (y <= 10); // true (chhota ya equal)
keyPoints: == equality check karta hai, != inequality check karta hai
Strings ke liye .equals() use karo, not ==
Hamesha boolean (true ya false) return hota hai

concepts.name: Logical Operators
concepts.explanation: Multiple conditions ko combine karo — jaise 'agar A bhi true ho AUR B bhi true ho'.
keyPoints: && (AND): Tab hi true jab dono true hon — strict hai boss
|| (OR): Ek bhi true ho to true — lenient hai
! (NOT): true ko false, false ko true — ulat deta hai

concepts.name: if / else if / else
concepts.explanation: Conditions check karo aur uske according code run karo. Jaise traffic light — red pe ruko, green pe chalo.
keyPoints: Conditions upar se neeche check hoti hain
Pehli true condition execute hoti hai, baaki skip
else block optional hai — default case ke liye

concepts.name: Ternary Operator
concepts.explanation: if-else ko ek line mein likho — shortcut hai! Jaise one-liner WhatsApp status.
keyPoints: Syntax: condition ? valueAgarTrue : valueAgarFalse
Simple if-else assignments ke liye use karo
Code chhota aur clean lagta hai

concepts.name: switch Statement
concepts.explanation: Multiple options mein se ek choose karo — jaise restaurant menu mein item select karna.
keyPoints: break lagao nahi to neeche wale cases bhi run ho jaayenge
default case unmatched values handle karta hai
int, char, String, enum ke saath kaam karta hai

concepts.name: for Loop
concepts.explanation: Ek kaam baar-baar karo fixed times — jaise subah 10 push-ups roz karte ho.
keyPoints: Teen parts: start; condition; update — semicolon se separate
Har baar pehle condition check hoti hai
Tab use karo jab pata ho kitni baar karna hai

concepts.name: while Loop
concepts.explanation: Jab tak condition true hai tab tak karo — jaise jab tak bhookh na mite khate raho!
keyPoints: Pehle condition check hoti hai — false ho to ek baar bhi nahi chalega
Agar start mein hi false ho to body skip ho jaayegi
Jab pata nahi kitni baar karna hai tab use karo

concepts.name: do-while Loop
concepts.explanation: Kam se kam ek baar to chale hi — jaise ATM PIN: pehle enter karo, phir check hoga sahi hai ya nahi.
keyPoints: Pehle code chalta hai, phir condition check hoti hai
Guarantee hai ki kam se kam ek baar chalega
Input validation ke liye useful — jab tak sahi input na mile

concepts.name: for-each Loop
concepts.explanation: Array ya collection ke har element pe ek-ek karke jaao — jaise class mein har student ka naam lena.
keyPoints: Simple syntax — har element ko automatically mil jaata hai
Loop ke andar collection change mat karo
Index nahi milta — sirf element milta hai

concepts.name: break aur continue
concepts.explanation: Loop control karo — break se bahar niklo, continue se ek round skip karo. Jaise cricket mein: break = match chhod do, continue = ye ball chhodo, next ball khelo.
keyPoints: break pura loop chhod deta hai — game over
continue current round skip karke next pe jaata hai
Dono sirf nearest loop pe kaam karte hain (nested mein dhyaan)

concepts.name: Array Declaration & Initialization
concepts.explanation: Fixed-size collection jahan same type ke elements rehte hain — jaise train ke numbered seats. Har seat ka fixed number hai aur ek baar train bani to seats badha nahi sakte.
keyPoints: Array size fixed hai — ek baar bana to change nahi hoga
Index 0 se start hota hai — pehla element index 0 pe
.length property use karo size ke liye (method nahi hai)
Galat index access karoge to ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException

concepts.name: Multi-dimensional Arrays
concepts.explanation: Arrays ke andar arrays — jaise Excel sheet jahan rows aur columns hain. 2D array ek table hai.
keyPoints: 2D array = array of arrays — table samjho
Access karo [row][column] se
Jagged arrays bhi ho sakte hain (alag-alag size ki rows)

concepts.name: Classes aur Objects
concepts.explanation: Class ek cookie cutter hai — shape define karta hai. Object actual cookie hai jo uspe banti hai. Ek cutter se bahut saari cookies bana sakte ho, har ek apni color/flavor (data) ke saath. Class = blueprint, Object = real cheez.
keyPoints: Class = blueprint (define karta hai objects mein kya hoga)
Object = actual instance (real cheez apne data ke saath)
this = 'jo object abhi use ho raha hai' (confusion bachata hai)
Constructor = special method jo 'new' keyword pe call hota hai

concepts.name: Encapsulation
concepts.explanation: Bank vault ki tarah socho. Tum seedha vault mein haath nahi daal sakte — teller (methods) ke through jaate ho jo check karta hai tum allowed ho ya nahi. Encapsulation data chhupaata hai (private) aur access control karta hai getters/setters se.
keyPoints: private = sirf ye class access kar sakti hai (vault door)
public = koi bhi access kar sakta hai (teller window)
Getters bahar walo ko data safely READ karne dete hain
Setters bahar walo ko data safely WRITE karne dete hain with validation

concepts.name: Inheritance
concepts.explanation: Inheritance genetics jaisi hai — baccha parent se traits inherit karta hai. Java mein child class automatically parent ki public/protected cheezein le leti hai, phir apni cheezein add kar sakti hai. Code reuse free mein milta hai!
keyPoints: extends = 'ye class us class se inherit karti hai'
Child ko sab public/protected members automatically milte hain
Java mein single-inheritance hi hai (ek hi parent) — multiple ke liye interfaces use karo
super.method() parent wala method call karta hai

concepts.name: Polymorphism - Method Overriding
concepts.explanation: Child class parent ke method ko apne tarike se implement karti hai — jaise papa guitar bajate hain, beta bhi guitar bajata hai but apne style mein!
keyPoints: @Override annotation lagao — recommended hai
Method signature parent wala match hona chahiye
Konsa method chalega ye runtime pe decide hota hai (dynamic binding)

concepts.name: Polymorphism - Method Overloading
concepts.explanation: Same naam ke multiple methods but alag parameters — jaise chai banane ke tarike: chai(), chai(elaichi), chai(adrak), chai(elaichi, adrak). Same kaam, alag variations.
keyPoints: Same method name, alag parameters
Compile-time polymorphism — compile karte waqt decide hota hai
Sirf return type different ho to kaam nahi karega

concepts.name: Abstract Classes
concepts.explanation: Abstract class ek incomplete blueprint hai — iska object nahi ban sakta, but dusri classes isse extend karke complete kar sakti hain. Jaise 'Vehicle' abstract hai — asli mein car ya bike hoti hai, 'Vehicle' nahi.
keyPoints: Abstract class ka object nahi ban sakta
Abstract aur concrete dono methods ho sakte hain
Child class ko sab abstract methods implement karne padte hain
Tab use karo jab classes mein common behavior ho

concepts.name: Interfaces
concepts.explanation: Interface ek contract hai — batata hai ki class ko kya karna hai, but kaise karna hai ye nahi batata. Jaise government rule: 'Har vehicle mein horn hona chahiye' — kaise implement karo, wo tumpe hai.
keyPoints: Sab methods by default public aur abstract hain
Ek class multiple interfaces implement kar sakti hai
Multiple inheritance achieve hoti hai interfaces se
Java 8+: interfaces mein default methods bhi ho sakte hain

concepts.name: ArrayList
concepts.explanation: Dynamic array jo grow/shrink ho sakta hai — jaise rubber band. Array fixed hai train ki tarah, ArrayList bus ki tarah hai jo passengers ke hisaab se adjust ho jaaye.
keyPoints: Resizable array — khud adjust ho jaata hai
Insertion order maintain hota hai
Duplicates aur null allowed hain
Random access fast hai O(1) — seedha index se lo

concepts.name: HashMap
concepts.explanation: Key-value pairs store karo fast lookup ke liye — jaise phone book. Naam (key) se number (value) turant mil jaata hai. No need to search through everyone!
keyPoints: Key-value pairs store karta hai
Keys unique honi chahiye — duplicate key pe purani value overwrite
Lookup bahut fast hai O(1) average
Order guaranteed nahi hai

concepts.name: HashSet
concepts.explanation: Unique elements ka collection — duplicates allowed nahi. Jaise VIP party guest list — ek naam ek hi baar aa sakta hai!
keyPoints: Duplicate elements nahi — har cheez ek hi baar
Order guaranteed nahi hai
add/remove/contains sab fast hain O(1)
Ek null allowed hai

concepts.name: LinkedList
concepts.explanation: Doubly-linked list — har element apne aage-peeche wale se connected hai. Beech mein insert/delete fast hai (sirf links change karo), but random access slow.
keyPoints: Ends pe insert/delete efficient hai
Random access ArrayList se slow hai
Stack ya Queue ki tarah bhi use ho sakti hai
Har element ke liye extra memory lagti hai (links ke liye)

concepts.name: try-catch-finally
concepts.explanation: Errors handle karo bina crash hue — jaise agar kuch toot jaaye to plan B ready rakho! try mein risky code, catch mein backup plan, finally mein cleanup.
keyPoints: try: Risky code jo exception throw kar sakta hai
catch: Specific exception handle karo
finally: Hamesha chalta hai (cleanup code ke liye)
Multiple catch blocks se alag-alag exceptions handle karo

concepts.name: throw aur throws
concepts.explanation: throw se manually exception fenko, throws se batao ki method exception throw kar sakta hai. Jaise — throw: bomb phenko, throws: warning label lagao.
keyPoints: throw: Actually exception fenkta hai
throws: Method signature mein declare karta hai
Checked exceptions ko ya to handle karo ya declare karo
RuntimeException ke liye throws lagana zaroori nahi

concepts.name: Custom Exceptions
concepts.explanation: Apne exceptions banao specific errors ke liye — jaise InvalidAgeException, InsufficientFundsException. Isse code readable hota hai.
keyPoints: Exception extend karo checked exceptions ke liye
RuntimeException extend karo unchecked ke liye
Meaningful error messages do
Domain-specific errors ke liye use karo

concepts.name: String Methods
concepts.explanation: String pe common operations — length, trim, uppercase, lowercase, substring, replace, split, contains. String immutable hai yani ek baar bani to change nahi hoti, har operation new string banata hai.
keyPoints: Strings immutable hain — change nahi hoti
Methods new String object return karte hain
Comparison ke liye .equals() use karo, == nahi
Bahut saari concatenation ke liye StringBuilder use karo

concepts.name: StringBuilder
concepts.explanation: Mutable string jo modify ho sakti hai efficiently — jab bahut saari string operations karne hon tab use karo. Naya object nahi banta, same object modify hota hai.
keyPoints: Mutable hai — modify ho sakta hai
Loops mein concatenation ke liye efficient
Thread-safe nahi (StringBuffer thread-safe hai)
Methods same object modify karte hain in-place

concepts.name: Stream Operations
concepts.explanation: Functional-style operations collections pe — filter, map, reduce. Conveyor belt ki tarah socho: data aata hai, process hota hai, result nikalta hai. Code bahut clean aur readable hota hai.
keyPoints: filter: Condition match karne wale select karo
map: Har element transform karo
reduce: Sab combine karke ek value banao
Streams lazy hain aur chain ho sakte hain

concepts.name: Lambda Syntax
concepts.explanation: Anonymous functions ka short form — inline function likho bina pura class banaye. Arrow (=>) notation use hota hai. Bahut kam code mein bahut kaam!
keyPoints: Syntax: (parameters) -> expression
Multiple statements ke liye {} use karo
Parameter types ka inference automatic hota hai
Functional interfaces ke saath kaam karta hai

concepts.name: Generic Classes
concepts.explanation: Type-safe classes banao jo kisi bhi type ke saath kaam karein — jaise ek dabba jo integers bhi rakh sake aur strings bhi. Type compile time pe decide hota hai, runtime pe nahi.
keyPoints: T type parameter hai (koi bhi naam ho sakta hai)
Compile time pe type safety deta hai
Casting ki zaroorat nahi
Common conventions: T (Type), E (Element), K (Key), V (Value)

concepts.name: Generic Methods
concepts.explanation: Methods jo alag-alag types ke saath kaam karein — same logic, different types.
keyPoints: Type parameter return type se pehle aata hai
Type arguments se infer hota hai
Multiple type parameters ho sakte hain
Code reusability badhti hai

concepts.name: Bounded Type Parameters
concepts.explanation: Generic types ko restrict karo — sirf specific types allowed hongi. extends keyword se upper bound lagao, jaise sirf Numbers accept karo Strings nahi.
keyPoints: extends bound type ko ek specific class/interface tak limit karta hai
Bound type ke methods access kar sakte ho
Multiple bounds: T extends Class & Interface1 & Interface2
Generic types pe specific operations enable karta hai

concepts.name: Wildcards
concepts.explanation: Flexible type parameters — exact type jaane bina kaam karo. ? extends Number matlab koi bhi Number ka subtype le lo, chahe Integer ho ya Double.
keyPoints: ? unknown type represent karta hai
? extends T: upper bounded — read karo, write mat karo
? super T: lower bounded — write karo, read careful se karo
Jab exact type jaanna zaroori na ho tab use karo

concepts.name: Threads Banana
concepts.explanation: Java mein threads banane ke do tarike hain — Thread extend karo ya Runnable implement karo. Thread ek alag execution path hai — multiple kaam simultaneously kar sakte ho.
keyPoints: Runnable interface preferred hai Thread extend karne se
start() call karo, run() nahi — run() se naya thread nahi banta
start() naya thread create karta hai, run() current mein hi chalta hai
Lambda se concise thread creation

concepts.name: Thread Synchronization
concepts.explanation: Race conditions rokne ke liye synchronized use karo — jab multiple threads same data access karein. Jaise bathroom ka lock — ek time pe ek hi jaaye!
keyPoints: synchronized concurrent access rokta hai
Methods ya blocks dono pe laga sakte ho
Object ke monitor pe synchronize hota hai
join() thread complete hone ka wait karta hai

concepts.name: ExecutorService
concepts.explanation: Thread pool manage karo efficiently. ExecutorService ek pool maintain karta hai threads ka — har baar naya thread banane ki zaroorat nahi. Callable se result bhi wapas le sakte ho!
keyPoints: ExecutorService thread pool manage karta hai
newFixedThreadPool(n): Fixed n threads
newCachedThreadPool(): Zaroorat ke hisaab se threads badhte hain
Callable result return karta hai, Runnable nahi karta
Future.get() result retrieve karta hai (blocking)

concepts.name: CompletableFuture
concepts.explanation: Modern async programming! Chain operations banao, multiple futures combine karo — sab non-blocking. Jaise pizza order — wait mat karo, callback pe kaam karo!
keyPoints: supplyAsync(): Task async mein chalao
thenApply(): Result transform karo
thenAccept(): Result consume karo (void return)
thenCombine(): Multiple futures combine karo
exceptionally(): Exceptions handle karo

concepts.name: TreeMap
concepts.explanation: HashMap ki tarah hai, but keys automatically sorted rehti hain! Red-Black tree use karta hai internaly. Jab alphabetically ya numerically sorted data chahiye tab perfect.
keyPoints: Keys natural order ya Comparator se sort hoti hain
O(log n) for add, remove, contains
firstKey(), lastKey(), subMap() jaise NavigableMap operations
Null keys allowed nahi

concepts.name: TreeSet
concepts.explanation: HashSet ki tarah hai but elements sorted rehte hain aur duplicates allowed nahi. Sorted unique collection chahiye? TreeSet perfect choice hai!
keyPoints: Elements natural order mein sorted rehte hain
Duplicates allowed nahi
O(log n) operations
NavigableSet methods milte hain

concepts.name: Queue aur Deque
concepts.explanation: Queue FIFO hai — jaise line mein khade hona, pehle aaya pehle jaayega. Deque double-ended hai — dono taraf se add/remove. Stack aur Queue dono ki tarah use ho sakta hai!
keyPoints: Queue: FIFO (First In First Out)
offer/poll safe hain — exception nahi dete
Deque: Dono ends se insert/remove kar sakte ho
Stack ya Queue dono ki tarah use ho sakta hai

concepts.name: PriorityQueue
concepts.explanation: Priority ke hisaab se elements nikalta hai, insertion order se nahi! Default min-heap hai. Algorithms mein bahut use hota hai — Dijkstra, task scheduling, etc.
keyPoints: Elements natural order ya Comparator ke hisaab se ordered
offer aur poll O(log n)
peek O(1) — bina nikale dekho
Thread-safe nahi — concurrent use mein PriorityBlockingQueue use karo

concepts.name: File Padhna
concepts.explanation: Text files padhne ke multiple tarike hain. Chhoti files ke liye Files.readAllLines, badi files ke liye BufferedReader. Try-with-resources se file auto-close hoti hai!
keyPoints: Files.readAllLines: Simple, ek baar mein sab padhta hai
BufferedReader: Badi files ke liye efficient
Scanner: Flexible parsing ke liye
Try-with-resources se file auto-close hoti hai

concepts.name: File Likhna
concepts.explanation: Text files mein likhne ke tarike. Append mode true karne se existing file mein data add hota hai bina purania delete kiye.
keyPoints: Files.write: Chhoti files ke liye simple
BufferedWriter: Badi files ke liye better performance
Append mode (true) se existing file mein data add hota hai
Try-with-resources use karo writer band karne ke liye

concepts.name: File Operations
concepts.explanation: Files aur directories ke saath kaam — check karo, banao, mita do, copy karo, move karo. Modern NIO Files class use karna better hai.
keyPoints: File class se file/directory operations karo
exists(), isFile(), isDirectory() se checks
Files class modern NIO operations ke liye
mkdir() ek folder, mkdirs() nested folders banata hai

concepts.name: Serialization
concepts.explanation: Java objects ko file mein save karo aur baad mein wapas le aao! Serializable implement karo. transient keyword sensitive fields ko exclude karta hai.
keyPoints: Serializable interface implement karo
serialVersionUID version control ke liye zaroori hai
transient keyword field ko exclude karta hai
ObjectOutputStream/ObjectInputStream use karo

concepts.name: LocalDate, LocalTime, LocalDateTime
concepts.explanation: Java 8 ne puri date-time API naya likh di — pehli wali buggy thi! LocalDate sirf date, LocalTime sirf time, LocalDateTime dono. Sab immutable hain — operations naya object return karte hain.
keyPoints: LocalDate: Sirf date
LocalTime: Sirf time
LocalDateTime: Date + time, but timezone nahi
Immutable — operations naya object return karta hai
plusDays(), minusDays() se date arithmetic

concepts.name: ZonedDateTime aur Formatting
concepts.explanation: Global apps mein timezone handle karna zaroori hai! ZoneId se timezone specify karo. DateTimeFormatter se date format aur parse karo apne hisaab se.
keyPoints: ZonedDateTime: Date + time + timezone
withZoneSameInstant(): Timezone convert karo
DateTimeFormatter: Format aur parse karo
Global apps ke liye timezone handling zaroori hai

concepts.name: Period aur Duration
concepts.explanation: Do timepoints ke beech ka difference nikalo. Period date-based hai (din, mahine, saal), Duration time-based hai (ghante, minute, second).
keyPoints: Period: Date-based (din, mahine, saal)
Duration: Time-based (ghante, minute, second)
between() se difference nikalo
ChronoUnit.DAYS.between() se total days directly

concepts.name: Wrapper Classes aur Autoboxing
concepts.explanation: Har primitive type ka ek Wrapper class hai. Collections mein primitives directly nahi jaate, Wrappers jaate hain. Java automatically convert karta hai dono direction mein.
keyPoints: int->Integer, double->Double, char->Character, boolean->Boolean, etc.
Autoboxing: primitive se wrapper automatic convert
Unboxing: wrapper se primitive automatic convert
Comparison ke liye == mat use karo, equals() use karo!
parseInt() String ko primitive mein convert karta hai

concepts.name: Optional se Null Safety
concepts.explanation: NullPointerException Java developers ka sabse bada dushman! Optional ek container hai jo value hai ya nahi clearly communicate karta hai. null check bhoolne ki problem khatam.
keyPoints: Optional.of(): Value null nahi ho sakti
Optional.ofNullable(): Value null ho sakti hai
orElse(): Empty hone pe default value
orElseThrow(): Empty hone pe exception
map(), filter(): Optional mein hi transform karo
NullPointerException se bachata hai

concepts.name: Math Class
concepts.explanation: Mathematical operations ke liye Math class — sab static methods. PI, E constants, trig functions, power, roots — sab kuch. Math.random() basic ke liye, Random class advanced ke liye.
keyPoints: Sab methods static hain — object banane ki zaroorat nahi
Trig functions radians mein hain, degrees mein nahi
Math.random() return karta hai [0.0, 1.0)
1-N range: (int)(Math.random() * N) + 1

concepts.name: Random Class
concepts.explanation: Math.random() se zyada control chahiye? Random class use karo! Bounded integers, boolean, different distributions — sab milta hai. Seed se reproducible sequences banao.
keyPoints: nextInt(bound): 0 se bound-1 tak
Seed se reproducible sequences — testing ke liye perfect
Multithreading mein ThreadLocalRandom use karo
Collections.shuffle() list ko random order mein karo

concepts.name: var Keyword (Java 10+)
concepts.explanation: Java ko type figure out karne do — tumhe likhna nahi padta. Dynamic typing nahi hai — type compile time pe pata hai, sirf infer hota hai. Lambi generic types ke liye bahut helpful!
keyPoints: Type compile time pe decide hota hai — strongly typed hai!
Sirf local variables ke liye jinka initializer ho
Code clean lagta hai jab type right side se obvious ho
Avoid karo jab type clear na ho: var x = getValue(); // x kya hai?

concepts.name: Records (Java 16+)
concepts.explanation: Data classes ke liye boilerplate likhne se tang aa gaye? Records Java ka shortcut hai 'bas ye data hold karo' ke liye. Constructor, getters, equals(), hashCode(), toString() sab FREE mein milte hain!
keyPoints: Records immutable hain — fields final hain, setters nahi hote
Auto-generate: constructor, getters, equals(), hashCode(), toString()
Getters field name use karte hain (getField() nahi) — e.g., person.name()
DTOs, API responses, value objects ke liye perfect

concepts.name: Text Blocks (Java 15+)
concepts.explanation: Multi-line strings bina escape character ke nightmare ke! Finally JSON, SQL, ya HTML likhte waqt readable code milta hai! Opening """ ke baad new line honi chahiye.
keyPoints: Opening """ ke baad newline honi chahiye
Indentation closing """ ke relative hoti hai
Andar quotes escape karne ki zaroorat nahi
.formatted() se string interpolation karo

concepts.name: Pattern Matching instanceof (Java 16+)
concepts.explanation: Pehle instanceof check karo, phir cast karo — ye double kaam tha. Ab Java ek step mein karta hai! Variable automatically cast ho jaata hai. Boilerplate zero!
keyPoints: Variable automatically cast aur available ho jaata hai
Boilerplate aur ClassCastException risk dono khatam
&& conditions ke saath kaam karta hai, || se nahi
Future Java mein aur bhi pattern matching features aayenge

concepts.name: Sealed Classes (Java 17+)
concepts.explanation: Exactly kitni subclasses hongi ye control karo. Invite-only inheritance! Compiler ko sab subtypes pata hain toh exhaustive switch bhi kara sako — default ki zaroorat nahi.
keyPoints: sealed = sirf ye specific classes extend kar sakti hain
Permitted subclasses final, sealed ya non-sealed honi chahiye
Exhaustive pattern matching switch mein enable karta hai
Fixed types ke sets ke liye best (jaise AST nodes)
